svg.text: Local Projectile
Client-side entities for projectile and on-hit effects
I. Definition of Local Projectiles
Local projectiles are entities computed and rendered locally (client-side) to simulate projectile effects and on-hit effects.
Must be predefined during editing before being referenced and created by skill node graphs.
Runtime depends on projectile motion device (movement rules) and on-hit detection (collision trigger rules).
II. Client Bullets vs Server Bullets
Local Projectile
"Client Bullet" - computed locally
Created via skill config client node graphs
Uses Fixed-Point Projectile Launch node
Projectile Motion Device
"Server Bullet" - computed by server
Created via server node graphs dynamically
Uses Create Projectile node
III. Editing Local Projectiles
Combat Preset > Local Projectile tab
Naming and Indexing:
Each name must be globally unique. A globally unique Prefab ID is auto-generated.
Click "Confirm Create" to create a new local projectile.
IV. Base Attributes (Column 1)
Basic Settings
Model from projectile model library provided by editor.
Supports X, Y, Z axis scaling. Zoom values persist after switching models.
Combat Parameters
Attribute Settings
Inherited from Creator
or Independent (configure HP, ATK, DEF as needed)
Whether Subsequent Settings Are Influenced by Creator
Life Cycle Settings
Permanent Duration
If off, specify duration time
Duration
Max time from init to destruction
X/Z Axis Destruction Distance
End of Life Cycle Behavior
When life cycle ends, executes configured ability units in order from top to bottom.
Y-Axis Destruction Distance also applies. X/Z distance is highest priority destroy condition (except Ability Units).
V. Common Components (Column 2)
VFX Playing
Visual effects component for the
projectile's appearance
Projectile Motion Device
Determines runtime movement rules
(linear, parabolic, tracking)
On-Hit Detection
Determines collision trigger rules
and effects on impact
VI. Ability Units (Column 3)
Local projectiles can reference predefined ability units. Adjust through "Advanced Editing".
In On-Hit Detection component, predefined ability units can be referenced and executed once hit detection is triggered.
Ability units require predefined editing. See Ability Units documentation for details.
VII. Tutorial: Parabolic Trajectory Bullet (Server Bullet)
Feature: Create a bullet with parabolic trajectory using Projectile Motion Device
Create prefab as bullet (empty object), add Projectile Motion Device component
Select "Parabolic Projectile". Set Initial Velocity: x=0, y=8, z=4 (60-degree launch angle)
Speed auto-converts from initial velocity. Gravitational acceleration affects trajectory/distance.
Add looping VFX for bullet visibility. Click "Preview Current Motion" to verify trajectory.
Node graph: Event "When Tab Selected" -> Binary Branch -> Check Option ID = 1 -> Create Projectile
Mount node graph on turret, place prefabs in scene, test gameplay
VIII. Tutorial: Blade Wave Skill (Client Bullet)
Feature: Create a skill that emits blade waves using Local Projectiles
Combat Preset > Local Projectiles > Create new. Select "Empty Model" for blade wave VFX.
Combat Parameters: Inherited from Creator. Life Cycle: Permanent enabled. XYZ Destroy Distance: 50.00
VFX: Select loop effect + blade wave asset. Projectile Motion Device: Linear Projectile, accel rate/duration = 5
Hit Detection: Configure area matching effect width. Trigger Type: non-repeatable. Detection Delay: 0.5
Ability Unit: Create "Direct Attack" ability unit with damage values
Skill animation: New Skill > "Sword Normal Attack" > Status Track: VFX Playing "Sword"
Skill node graph: Get attach point position/rotation > Fixed-Point Projectile Launch (use custom "FirePoint")
Insert logic into node graph event track at corresponding frame. Equip skill in class interface and test.
Warning: Do not set bullet speed too high. Bullets moving too fast may pass through creations
(monsters) without dealing damage. Set Detection Delay to avoid self-detection on creation.
IX. Extended: Loot Drop Visuals After Opening Chest
Solution: Projectile A hits ground -> get coordinates -> generate entity B at landing position
Create prefab A with projectile component, configure parabolic motion
Configure hit detection, disable scene/water surface ignoring to get ground hit coordinates
Create prefab B with pickup logic (same appearance as A, different logic for player pickup)
Chest logic: generate 5 prefab A when entering collision box
Prefab A node graph: "When Hit Detection Triggers" -> get hit position -> check if scene -> generate prefab B
Tip: The projectile motion device takes effect immediately when the object is created by default.
Other projectile types: Linear Projectile and Following Projectile. See Projectile Motion Device guide for details.
